verses.latin_vulgate: Visio Isaiæ, filii Amos, quam vidit super Judam et Jerusalem, in diebus Oziæ, Joathan, Achaz, et Ezechiæ, regum Juda.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חֲזוֹן֙ יְשַֽׁעְיָ֣הוּ בֶן־אָמ֔וֹץ אֲשֶׁ֣ר חָזָ֔ה עַל־יְהוּדָ֖ה וִירֽוּשָׁלָ֑͏ִם בִּימֵ֨י עֻזִּיָּ֧הוּ יוֹתָ֛ם אָחָ֥ז יְחִזְקִיָּ֖הוּ מַלְכֵ֥י יְהוּדָֽה׃
verses.king_james: The vision of Isaiah the son of Amoz, which he saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem in the days of Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, kings of Judah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audite, cæli, et auribus percipe, terra,  quoniam Dominus locutus est.  Filios enutrivi, et exaltavi ;  ipsi autem spreverunt me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁמְע֤וּ שָׁמַ֙יִם֙ וְהַאֲזִ֣ינִי אֶ֔רֶץ כִּ֥י יְהֹוָ֖ה דִּבֵּ֑ר בָּנִים֙ גִּדַּ֣לְתִּי וְרוֹמַ֔מְתִּי וְהֵ֖ם פָּ֥שְׁעוּ בִֽי׃
verses.king_james: Hear, O heavens, and give ear, O earth: for the Lord hath spoken, I have nourished and brought up children, and they have rebelled against me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cognovit bos possessorem suum,  et asinus præsepe domini sui ;  Israël autem me non cognovit,  et populus meus non intellexit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָדַ֥ע שׁוֹר֙ קֹנֵ֔הוּ וַחֲמ֖וֹר אֵב֣וּס בְּעָלָ֑יו יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ לֹ֣א יָדַ֔ע עַמִּ֖י לֹ֥א הִתְבּוֹנָֽן׃
verses.king_james: The ox knoweth his owner, and the ass his master’s crib: but Israel doth not know, my people doth not consider.

verses.latin_vulgate: Væ genti peccatrici,  populo gravi iniquitate,  semini nequam, filiis sceleratis !  dereliquerunt Dominum ;  blasphemaverunt Sanctum Israël ;  abalienati sunt retrorsum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֣וֹי ׀ גּ֣וֹי חֹטֵ֗א עַ֚ם כֶּ֣בֶד עָוֺ֔ן זֶ֣רַע מְרֵעִ֔ים בָּנִ֖ים מַשְׁחִיתִ֑ים עָזְב֣וּ אֶת־יְהֹוָ֗ה נִֽאֲצ֛וּ אֶת־קְד֥וֹשׁ יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל נָזֹ֥רוּ אָחֽוֹר׃
verses.king_james: Ah sinful nation, a people laden with iniquity, a seed of evildoers, children that are corrupters: they have forsaken the Lord, they have provoked the Holy One of Israel unto anger, they are gone away backward.

verses.latin_vulgate: Super quo percutiam vos ultra, addentes prævaricationem ?  omne caput languidum,  et omne cor mœrens.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַ֣ל מֶ֥ה תֻכּ֛וּ ע֖וֹד תּוֹסִ֣יפוּ סָרָ֑ה כׇּל־רֹ֣אשׁ לׇֽחֳלִ֔י וְכׇל־לֵבָ֖ב דַּוָּֽי׃
verses.king_james: Why should ye be stricken any more? ye will revolt more and more: the whole head is sick, and the whole heart faint.

verses.latin_vulgate: A planta pedis usque ad verticem,  non est in eo sanitas ;  vulnus, et livor, et plaga tumens,  non est circumligata, nec curata medicamine,  neque fota oleo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִכַּף־רֶ֤גֶל וְעַד־רֹאשׁ֙ אֵֽין־בּ֣וֹ מְתֹ֔ם פֶּ֥צַע וְחַבּוּרָ֖ה וּמַכָּ֣ה טְרִיָּ֑ה לֹא־זֹ֙רוּ֙ וְלֹ֣א חֻבָּ֔שׁוּ וְלֹ֥א רֻכְּכָ֖ה בַּשָּֽׁמֶן׃
verses.king_james: From the sole of the foot even unto the head there is no soundness in it; but wounds, and bruises, and putrifying sores: they have not been closed, neither bound up, neither mollified with ointment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Terra vestra deserta ;  civitates vestræ succensæ igni :  regionem vestram coram vobis alieni devorant,  et desolabitur sicut in vastitate hostili.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַרְצְכֶ֣ם שְׁמָמָ֔ה עָרֵיכֶ֖ם שְׂרֻפ֣וֹת אֵ֑שׁ אַדְמַתְכֶ֗ם לְנֶגְדְּכֶם֙ זָרִים֙ אֹכְלִ֣ים אֹתָ֔הּ וּשְׁמָמָ֖ה כְּמַהְפֵּכַ֥ת זָרִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Your country is desolate, your cities are burned with fire: your land, strangers devour it in your presence, and it is desolate, as overthrown by strangers.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et derelinquetur filia Sion  ut umbraculum in vinea,  et sicut tugurium in cucumerario,  et sicut civitas quæ vastatur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנוֹתְרָ֥ה בַת־צִיּ֖וֹן כְּסֻכָּ֣ה בְכָ֑רֶם כִּמְלוּנָ֥ה בְמִקְשָׁ֖ה כְּעִ֥יר נְצוּרָֽה׃
verses.king_james: And the daughter of Zion is left as a cottage in a vineyard, as a lodge in a garden of cucumbers, as a besieged city.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nisi Dominus exercituum  reliquisset nobis semen,  quasi Sodoma fuissemus,  et quasi Gomorrha similes essemus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לוּלֵי֙ יְהֹוָ֣ה צְבָא֔וֹת הוֹתִ֥יר לָ֛נוּ שָׂרִ֖יד כִּמְעָ֑ט כִּסְדֹ֣ם הָיִ֔ינוּ לַעֲמֹרָ֖ה דָּמִֽינוּ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Except the Lord of hosts had left unto us a very small remnant, we should have been as Sodom, and we should have been like unto Gomorrah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audite verbum Domini,  principes Sodomorum ;  percipite auribus legem Dei nostri,  populus Gomorrhæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁמְע֥וּ דְבַר־יְהֹוָ֖ה קְצִינֵ֣י סְדֹ֑ם הַאֲזִ֛ינוּ תּוֹרַ֥ת אֱלֹהֵ֖ינוּ עַ֥ם עֲמֹרָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Hear the word of the Lord, ye rulers of Sodom; give ear unto the law of our God, ye people of Gomorrah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quo mihi multitudinem victimarum vestrarum ?  dicit Dominus.  Plenus sum :  holocausta arietum,  et adipem pinguium,  et sanguinem vitulorum et agnorum et hircorum,  nolui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָמָּה־לִּ֤י רֹב־זִבְחֵיכֶם֙ יֹאמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה שָׂבַ֛עְתִּי עֹל֥וֹת אֵילִ֖ים וְחֵ֣לֶב מְרִיאִ֑ים וְדַ֨ם פָּרִ֧ים וּכְבָשִׂ֛ים וְעַתּוּדִ֖ים לֹ֥א חָפָֽצְתִּי׃
verses.king_james: To what purpose is the multitude of your sacrifices unto me? saith the Lord: I am full of the burnt offerings of rams, and the fat of fed beasts; and I delight not in the blood of bullocks, or of lambs, or of he goats.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum veniretis ante conspectum meum,  quis quæsivit hæc de manibus vestris,  ut ambularetis in atriis meis ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י תָבֹ֔אוּ לֵֽרָא֖וֹת פָּנָ֑י מִֽי־בִקֵּ֥שׁ זֹ֛את מִיֶּדְכֶ֖ם רְמֹ֥ס חֲצֵרָֽי׃
verses.king_james: When ye come to appear before me, who hath required this at your hand, to tread my courts?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne offeratis ultra sacrificium frustra :  incensum abominatio est mihi.  Neomeniam et sabbatum, et festivitates alias, non feram ;  iniqui sunt cœtus vestri.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹ֣א תוֹסִ֗יפוּ הָבִיא֙ מִנְחַת־שָׁ֔וְא קְטֹ֧רֶת תּוֹעֵבָ֛ה הִ֖יא לִ֑י חֹ֤דֶשׁ וְשַׁבָּת֙ קְרֹ֣א מִקְרָ֔א לֹא־אוּכַ֥ל אָ֖וֶן וַעֲצָרָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Bring no more vain oblations; incense is an abomination unto me; the new moons and sabbaths, the calling of assemblies, I cannot away with; it is iniquity, even the solemn meeting.

verses.latin_vulgate: Calendas vestras, et solemnitates vestras odivit anima mea :  facta sunt mihi molesta ; laboravi sustinens.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חׇדְשֵׁיכֶ֤ם וּמֽוֹעֲדֵיכֶם֙ שָֽׂנְאָ֣ה נַפְשִׁ֔י הָי֥וּ עָלַ֖י לָטֹ֑רַח נִלְאֵ֖יתִי נְשֹֽׂא׃
verses.king_james: Your new moons and your appointed feasts my soul hateth: they are a trouble unto me; I am weary to bear them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum extenderitis manus vestras, avertam oculos meos a vobis,  et cum multiplicaveritis orationem, non exaudiam :  manus enim vestræ sanguine plenæ sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבְפָרִשְׂכֶ֣ם כַּפֵּיכֶ֗ם אַעְלִ֤ים עֵינַי֙ מִכֶּ֔ם גַּ֛ם כִּֽי־תַרְבּ֥וּ תְפִלָּ֖ה אֵינֶ֣נִּי שֹׁמֵ֑עַ יְדֵיכֶ֖ם דָּמִ֥ים מָלֵֽאוּ׃
verses.king_james: And when ye spread forth your hands, I will hide mine eyes from you: yea, when ye make many prayers, I will not hear: your hands are full of blood.

verses.latin_vulgate: Lavamini, mundi estote ;  auferte malum cogitationum vestrarum  ab oculis meis :  quiescite agere perverse,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רַֽחֲצוּ֙ הִזַּכּ֔וּ הָסִ֛ירוּ רֹ֥עַ מַעַלְלֵיכֶ֖ם מִנֶּ֣גֶד עֵינָ֑י חִדְל֖וּ הָרֵֽעַ׃
verses.king_james: Wash you, make you clean; put away the evil of your doings from before mine eyes; cease to do evil;

verses.latin_vulgate: discite benefacere ;  quærite judicium, subvenite oppresso,  judicate pupillo, defendite viduam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לִמְד֥וּ הֵיטֵ֛ב דִּרְשׁ֥וּ מִשְׁפָּ֖ט אַשְּׁר֣וּ חָמ֑וֹץ שִׁפְט֣וּ יָת֔וֹם רִ֖יבוּ אַלְמָנָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Learn to do well; seek judgment, relieve the oppressed, judge the fatherless, plead for the widow.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et venite, et arguite me, dicit Dominus.  Si fuerint peccata vestra ut coccinum,  quasi nix dealbabuntur ;  et si fuerint rubra quasi vermiculus,  velut lana alba erunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְכוּ־נָ֛א וְנִוָּכְחָ֖ה יֹאמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֑ה אִם־יִהְי֨וּ חֲטָאֵיכֶ֤ם כַּשָּׁנִים֙ כַּשֶּׁ֣לֶג יַלְבִּ֔ינוּ אִם־יַאְדִּ֥ימוּ כַתּוֹלָ֖ע כַּצֶּ֥מֶר יִֽהְיֽוּ׃
verses.king_james: Come now, and let us reason together, saith the Lord: though your sins be as scarlet, they shall be as white as snow; though they be red like crimson, they shall be as wool.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si volueritis, et audieritis me,  bona terræ comeditis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־תֹּאב֖וּ וּשְׁמַעְתֶּ֑ם ט֥וּב הָאָ֖רֶץ תֹּאכֵֽלוּ׃
verses.king_james: If ye be willing and obedient, ye shall eat the good of the land:

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod si nolueritis, et me ad iracundiam provocaveritis,  gladius devorabit vos,  quia os Domini locutum est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִם־תְּמָאֲנ֖וּ וּמְרִיתֶ֑ם חֶ֣רֶב תְּאֻכְּל֔וּ כִּ֛י פִּ֥י יְהֹוָ֖ה דִּבֵּֽר׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: But if ye refuse and rebel, ye shall be devoured with the sword: for the mouth of the Lord hath spoken it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo facta est meretrix  civitas fidelis, plena judicii ?  justitia habitavit in ea,  nunc autem homicidæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵיכָה֙ הָיְתָ֣ה לְזוֹנָ֔ה קִרְיָ֖ה נֶאֱמָנָ֑ה מְלֵאֲתִ֣י מִשְׁפָּ֗ט צֶ֛דֶק יָלִ֥ין בָּ֖הּ וְעַתָּ֥ה מְרַצְּחִֽים׃
verses.king_james: How is the faithful city become an harlot! it was full of judgment; righteousness lodged in it; but now murderers.

verses.latin_vulgate: Argentum tuum versum est in scoriam ;  vinum tuum mistum est aqua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כַּסְפֵּ֖ךְ הָיָ֣ה לְסִיגִ֑ים סׇבְאֵ֖ךְ מָה֥וּל בַּמָּֽיִם׃
verses.king_james: Thy silver is become dross, thy wine mixed with water:

verses.latin_vulgate: Principes tui infideles,  socii furum.  Omnes diligunt munera,  sequuntur retributiones.  Pupillo non judicant,  et causa viduæ non ingreditur ad illos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שָׂרַ֣יִךְ סוֹרְרִ֗ים וְחַבְרֵי֙ גַּנָּבִ֔ים כֻּלּוֹ֙ אֹהֵ֣ב שֹׁ֔חַד וְרֹדֵ֖ף שַׁלְמֹנִ֑ים יָתוֹם֙ לֹ֣א יִשְׁפֹּ֔טוּ וְרִ֥יב אַלְמָנָ֖ה לֹא־יָב֥וֹא אֲלֵיהֶֽם׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Thy princes are rebellious, and companions of thieves: every one loveth gifts, and followeth after rewards: they judge not the fatherless, neither doth the cause of the widow come unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter hoc ait Dominus,  Deus exercituum, Fortis Israël :  Heu ! consolabor super hostibus meis,  et vindicabor de inimicis meis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֗ן נְאֻ֤ם הָאָדוֹן֙ יְהֹוָ֣ה צְבָא֔וֹת אֲבִ֖יר יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל ה֚וֹי אֶנָּחֵ֣ם מִצָּרַ֔י וְאִנָּקְמָ֖ה מֵאוֹיְבָֽי׃
verses.king_james: Therefore saith the Lord, the Lord of hosts, the mighty One of Israel, Ah, I will ease me of mine adversaries, and avenge me of mine enemies:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et convertam manum meam ad te,  et excoquam ad puram scoriam tuam,  et auferam omne stannum tuum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאָשִׁ֤יבָה יָדִי֙ עָלַ֔יִךְ וְאֶצְרֹ֥ף כַּבֹּ֖ר סִיגָ֑יִךְ וְאָסִ֖ירָה כׇּל־בְּדִילָֽיִךְ׃
verses.king_james: And I will turn my hand upon thee, and purely purge away thy dross, and take away all thy tin:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et restituam judices tuos ut fuerunt prius,  et consiliarios tuos sicut antiquitus ;  post hæc vocaberis civitas justi,  urbs fidelis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאָשִׁ֤יבָה שֹׁפְטַ֙יִךְ֙ כְּבָרִ֣אשֹׁנָ֔ה וְיֹעֲצַ֖יִךְ כְּבַתְּחִלָּ֑ה אַֽחֲרֵי־כֵ֗ן יִקָּ֤רֵא לָךְ֙ עִ֣יר הַצֶּ֔דֶק קִרְיָ֖ה נֶאֱמָנָֽה׃
verses.king_james: And I will restore thy judges as at the first, and thy counsellors as at the beginning: afterward thou shalt be called, The city of righteousness, the faithful city.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sion in judicio redimetur,  et reducent eam in justitia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: צִיּ֖וֹן בְּמִשְׁפָּ֣ט תִּפָּדֶ֑ה וְשָׁבֶ֖יהָ בִּצְדָקָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Zion shall be redeemed with judgment, and her converts with righteousness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et conteret scelestos, et peccatores simul ;  et qui dereliquerunt Dominum consumentur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשֶׁ֧בֶר פֹּשְׁעִ֛ים וְחַטָּאִ֖ים יַחְדָּ֑ו וְעֹזְבֵ֥י יְהֹוָ֖ה יִכְלֽוּ׃
verses.king_james: And the destruction of the transgressors and of the sinners shall be together, and they that forsake the Lord shall be consumed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Confundentur enim ab idolis quibus sacrificaverunt,  et erubescetis super hortis quos elegeratis,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י יֵבֹ֔שׁוּ מֵאֵילִ֖ים אֲשֶׁ֣ר חֲמַדְתֶּ֑ם וְתַ֨חְפְּר֔וּ מֵהַגַּנּ֖וֹת אֲשֶׁ֥ר בְּחַרְתֶּֽם׃
verses.king_james: For they shall be ashamed of the oaks which ye have desired, and ye shall be confounded for the gardens that ye have chosen.

verses.latin_vulgate: cum fueritis velut quercus defluentibus foliis,  et velut hortus absque aqua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י תִֽהְי֔וּ כְּאֵלָ֖ה נֹבֶ֣לֶת עָלֶ֑הָ וּֽכְגַנָּ֔ה אֲשֶׁר־מַ֖יִם אֵ֥ין לָֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: For ye shall be as an oak whose leaf fadeth, and as a garden that hath no water.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erit fortitudo vestra ut favilla stuppæ,  et opus vestrum quasi scintilla,  et succendetur utrumque simul,  et non erit qui extinguat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָ֤ה הֶחָסֹן֙ לִנְעֹ֔רֶת וּפֹעֲל֖וֹ לְנִיצ֑וֹץ וּבָעֲר֧וּ שְׁנֵיהֶ֛ם יַחְדָּ֖ו וְאֵ֥ין מְכַבֶּֽה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: And the strong shall be as tow, and the maker of it as a spark, and they shall both burn together, and none shall quench them.
